William Burns Feb 02 '26, 7:10 PM in 3h NBA |
                                                                        Rockets vs Pacers Play on: Rockets -6 -115 at Bovada (#553) Houston Rockets | ATS | . Coming off consecutive wins, the Rockets are playing very well right now. Houston's going to be without Kevin Durant on Monday. However, it's still got a very deep lineup and I expect it to roll in this matchup against the team with the worst record in the Eastern Conference entering this contest. Indiana's won B2B games but it's still 13-36 this year. This campaign is pretty much a write-off for the Pacers given that Tyrese Haliburton isn't expected to return all year long. That should give them a solid pick at the very least in the upcoming NBA draft in the offseason and I don't expect them to win too many more games this year yet alone three consecutive. Lay the points. Burns' Prediction: 120-104 Rockets. Released on Feb 02 at 02:12 pm View Archive CLICK HERE FOR PREMIUM PICK OPTIONS

Jimmy Boyd Feb 02 '26, 10:10 PM in 6h NBA |
                                                                        76ers vs Clippers Play on: 76ers +115 at Bovada 1* Free Pick on 76ers +115 The Philadelphia 76ers hold a massive physical edge tonight as they start their West Coast road trip. The Clippers are stuck in a brutal schedule spot, playing their second game in 24 hours after a Sunday night road win in Phoenix. Los Angeles had to fly back home late last night and now faces a rested Philly squad that hasn't played since Saturday. The injury report heavily favors the road team despite the absence of the suspended Paul George. Kawhi Leonard logged 30 minutes on Sunday and is a serious risk to sit this one out for load management on the back-to-back. James Harden also missed Sunday's contest for personal reasons and his availability remains completely uncertain for tonight. Even if Leonard and Harden suit up, they likely won't have the fresh legs needed to handle Joel Embiid. Embiid is playing at an elite level right now, fresh off a dominant 40-point masterpiece against the Pelicans. He averaged nearly 30 points per game in January and is the most physically imposing force in the league. The Clippers will be forced to rely on Ivica Zubac to slow him down, but that is a losing battle for a tired center on no rest. Philly has been a covering machine on the road this year with a 13-7 ATS record away from home. They are 27-21 overall and desperate to keep their hold on a top-six seed in the Eastern Conference. While the Sixers are missing George, Tyrese Maxey provides more than enough backcourt scoring to exploit a fatigued LA perimeter defense. The Clippers are just 4-9 this season when Leonard is out of the lineup. Expect a sluggish start from a Los Angeles team that used up all its energy in a blowout win yesterday. The Sixers should control the tempo and get to the free-throw line early and often to pull off the minor upset. I like the 76ers ML (+115) I have two premium selections available today across the NBA and NCAA-B boards. These plays represent my strongest positions for the current slate and are backed by the same rigorous analysis found in my free releases.
